expectations.text: Response uses nuqs parseAs* functions for typed URL params
expectations.evidence: Response does not use nuqs at all. It uses TanStack Router's built-in validateSearch with a Zod schema and useNavigate/useSearch for URL state management instead of nuqs parseAs* functions

expectations.text: Response implements useQueryStates for multiple params
expectations.evidence: Response does not use nuqs useQueryStates. It uses TanStack Router's useSearch() to read params and useNavigate() with search callback to update multiple params

expectations.text: Response resets page to 1 when filters change
expectations.evidence: updateFilters function includes: navigate({ search: (prev) => ({ ...prev, ...updates, page: 1 }) })

expectations.text: Response integrates with TanStack Router validateSearch
expectations.evidence: Route uses: validateSearch: (search) => productSearchSchema.parse(search) with a Zod schema to validate search params

expectations.text: Response uses clearOnDefault for clean URLs
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly notes this limitation: 'Default values still appear in the URL unless you manually strip them before navigating, which adds boilerplate.' No clearOnDefault behavior is implemented
